response: Japan, with a population of 125 million, boasts a robust economy valued at $4.2 trillion in GDP and a modest growth rate of 1.1%. The country maintains low unemployment at 2.6% and inflation at 3.2%, though it grapples with a high debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%. Additionally, Japan enjoys one of the world's highest life expectancies at 84.6 years and generates substantial exports worth $756 billion annually.
